Motivational.title: The Eight Pillars of Success
Motivational.description: A motivational speech outlining the eight essential elements required for success: hard work, patience, sacrifice, consistency, discipline, self-confidence, positive attitude, and focus.

subtitles.text: Success demands eight things. Number one: hard work. Don't believe in luck; believe in hard work. Number two: patience. If
you are losing the patience, then you are losing the battle. Number three: sacrifice. If you don't sacrifice for what
you want, then what you want becomes the sacrifice. Number four: consistency. Consistency is what transforms average into excellence. Number
five: discipline. Gets you going, but discipline keeps you growing. Number six: self-confidence. Confidence is not 'they will
like me.' Confidence is 'I'll be fine if they don't like me.' Number seven: positive attitude. A positive attitude won't
solve all your problems, but it will annoy enough people to make it worth the effort. Focus on your goals,
not on your obstacles.

Motivational.title: The Struggle is Not Your Enemy
Motivational.description: A short speech about embracing life's challenges, using the metaphor of a diamond being shaped by pressure, heat, and time.

subtitles.text: The struggle is not your enemy; it's your teacher. It's in the struggle that you discover your strength. It's in
the struggle that you learn what you're truly made of. So when life knocks you down, don't stay down. Get
up, dust yourself off, and keep moving forward. Think of a diamond: it starts as a lump of coal buried
deep in the earth. It's only through pressure, heat, and time that it becomes something extraordinary. You are no different.
The challenges you face are not here to break you; they're here to shape you into the best version of
yourself.

Motivational.title: How to Overcome Fear - David Goggins
Motivational.description: David Goggins discusses overcoming fear by taking personal accountability, asking what is wrong, and fixing it by doing hard, uncomfortable things like joining the military.

subtitles.text: And I put it off on everybody else. It would until I say, 'You know what? For me to fix
this, I get a read: what the hell, what the fuck is wrong with David Goggins?' Not, 'don't blame anybody.
Read my book. It's okay, I'm afraid of my shadow. How can I overcome that? Go in the military, get
your ass kicked. Do things you hate to do. Be uncomfortable every day of your life. Roger that. I'm not
the smartest kid in the world. Okay, instead of somebody saying, 'Oh no, you're smart,' no, no, don't say that
to yourself. I said to myself, 'No, I'm dumb.' Okay, Roger that. How do you get smarter? Educate yourself.

Motivational.title: When You Have a Dream - Listen to This
Motivational.description: This motivational message explains that a dream is not something you actively seek, but something that sneaks up on you. It emphasizes that a dream or one's personal intuition always 'whispers' rather than shouts, making it essential to be ready to listen for it every day.

subtitles.text: When you have a dream, and the dream isn't something you dream and then it happens. The dream is something
you never knew was going to come into your life. Dreams always come from behind you, not, not right between
your eyes. It sneaks up on you. But when you have a dream, it doesn't often come at you screaming
in your face: 'This is who you are. This is what you must be for the rest of your life.'
Sometimes a dream almost whispers. And I've always said to my kids, the hardest thing to listen to your instincts,
your human personal intuition, always whispers. It never shouts. Very hard to hear. So you have to, every day of
your lives, be ready to hear what whispers in your ear. It very rarely shouts. And if you can listen
to the whisper, and if it tickles your heart, and it's something you think you want to do for the
rest of your life, then that is going to be what you do for the rest of your life. And
we will benefit from everything you do.

Motivational.title: This Speech By Rocky Will Change Your Life.
Motivational.description: In one of the most famous monologues in cinema history from 'Rocky Balboa' (2006), Rocky Balboa gives his son a powerful reality check. He explains that success isn't about how hard you can hit, but how much you can endure and still keep moving forward. He emphasizes personal responsibility and the courage to stop making excuses.

subtitles.text: The world ain't all sunshine and rainbows. It's a very mean and nasty place, and I don't care how tough you are,
it will beat you to your knees and keep you there permanently if you let it. You, me, or nobody is going to hit as hard as life.
But it ain't about how hard you hit; it's about how hard you can get hit and keep moving forward.
How much you can take and keep moving forward. That's how winning is done!
Now if you know what you're worth, now go out and get what you're worth. But you gotta be willing to take the hits,
and not pointing fingers saying you ain't where you want to be because of him, or her, or anybody!
Cowards do that, and that ain't you! You're better than that!

Motivational.title: ME VS ME - Motivational Speech
Motivational.description: A powerful motivational short by Ben Lionel Scott. The speech emphasizes personal accountability and internal growth, arguing that comparing yourself to others is a distraction. The core message is that the only competition that truly matters is with your former self and the effort you choose to put in today.

subtitles.text: I don't care who's doing better than me. I don't care what kind of results everyone else is getting.
I care what I'm doing. I care about the effort I'm putting in.
It's me versus me. No one else.

Motivational.title: You May Have Arms and Legs | Nick Vujicic Motivational Speech Video
Motivational.description: A powerful motivational clip featuring Nick Vujicic. He challenges the audience to define their worth, purpose, and destiny, asserting that lacking answers to these fundamental questions is a greater disability than his own physical condition of having no arms or legs.

subtitles.text: You may have arms and legs, but unless you know three things:
Number one: Who are you and what your value is?
Number two: What is your purpose here in life?
And number three: What is your destiny when you're done here?
If you don't know the answers to any of those three questions, you're more disabled than I am.

Motivational.title: How to Stay Focused and Disciplined
Motivational.description: Iman Gadzhi discusses the importance of discipline over motivation and focus. He argues that focus is not a glamorous or complex concept, but rather what remains when all distractions and noise are removed.

subtitles.text: How do you or how can you stay focused and disciplined for long periods of time?
Cut out the distractions. It's not about focus. Same thing, like, it's not about motivation; it's about discipline.
You know, it's not about these fancy, glamorous things. Focus... it's not about that.
If you cut out all distraction, the thing that's left is focus.
So cut out all the noise, and I think for once you'll actually enjoy working.

Motivational.title: Best Short Motivational Speech Video - 24 HOURS - 1-Minute Motivation #2
Motivational.description: A high-impact motivational speech emphasizing that success is determined by how one utilizes their 24 hours each day. The speaker compares the habits of successful icons like Oprah Winfrey to those who struggle, asserting that a person's daily schedule is the ultimate predictor of their future success.

subtitles.text: If you only have 24 hours in a day, your success is dependent upon how you use the 24.
You gotta hear me. People talk about Oprah Winfrey, Ted Turner, Warren Buffett. Listen to me. I don't care how much money you make.
You only get 24 hours in a day and the difference between Oprah and the person that's broke is Oprah uses her 24 hours wisely.

That's it, that's it. You can get only 24.I don't care if you grew up broke orI don't care if you grew up  rich, i don't care if you're in college, you are not in college. You only get 24 hours. And i blew up literally
I went from being a high school dropout to selling 6,000 books in less than six months. What happened in my 24 hours? I was like, okay, here you got to get a grip on your 24 hours

cause you bought to be broke for the rest of your life. And it just, all I need you to do for me. I can tell you all about your life. If you just write down your 24-hour schedule for me, you let me look at it. I can tell you where you're gonna be in five years. I can tell you where you're gonna be in ten years. I can tell you where you gonna be in 20 years, if you keep that schedule.

Motivational.title: Why not you? Jim Rohn
Motivational.description: Jim Rohn delivers a powerful motivational message, challenging the audience to take their potential personally. He emphasizes that with the ability to think, decide, and study, anyone can change their life, achieve their dreams, and build a secure future for their family.

subtitles.text: I want you to take it personal and my personal question to you is: why not you?
You've got the brains, you can make decisions, you can study the plan, you can change your life.
You can grow immensely in the next few years, you can make your dreams come true.
You can build a financial wall around your family, nothing can get through.
You can become healthy, you can become powerful. Why not you?

Motivational.title: It's So Attractive When Girls Dress Like This..
Motivational.description: Iman Gadzhi shares his personal preference for women's fashion, specifically describing a 'smart sexy' style. He appreciates clothing that is sophisticated and sometimes formal, suggesting that true confidence doesn't require revealing outfits.

subtitles.text: I like a girl who dresses smart, sexy.
And what I mean by that is she's dressed smart, almost sometimes formal.
And there's certain girls you look at and you can tell that they just have it.
They just... they don't need to show it off.
And I just find that incredibly, incredibly attractive.

Business.title: The Founding Idea of Amazon - Jeff Bezos
Business.description: Jeff Bezos explains that when he founded Amazon in 1994, he had to first explain what the internet was to investors. The founding idea was to build an online bookstore that could offer every book ever printed, which was impossible in a physical store.

subtitles.text: I started Amazon. It was 25 years ago. I started Amazon in 1994. When I went to seek investors, the first
question I had to answer is: what is the internet? None of my investors had ever heard of the internet.
And the idea that I had in mind was to build a bookstore. I realized that we could build
a store which would have every book ever printed in any language, in print or out of print. And we
could do that online. It would be impossible in a physical store, and that was the founding idea of Amazon.

Business.title: Elon Musk on Starting His First Company
Business.description: Elon Musk discusses the biggest surprise in his life, his desire to be involved in technology, and the unexpected reason he started a company: he couldn't get a job at the few internet companies available.

subtitles.text: What is the one thing that has surprised you about your life? I'm surprised by the whole thing, honestly. I
know I wanted to be involved in technology, um, and, uh, in fact, the only reason I started a company
back in '95, an internet company, was because I couldn't get it. There were only a few internet companies, and
I couldn't get a job at any of them. I tried to get a job at Netscape and sent my
resume, and I tried hanging out in the lobby, but I was too shy to talk to anyone. And, and
then I was like, okay, well, I guess I'll have to start a company because I can't get a job anywhere.

Business.title: $12B CEO How to become a millionaire by copying?
Business.description: In this podcast clip from Brexbits, Henrique Dubugras, the co-founder and co-CEO of Brex, explains the 'secret' of Silicon Valley innovation. He argues that most successful companies like Google, Facebook, and Tesla weren't the first in their fields; instead, they took existing ideas and added a small, better evolution. He highlights how even Meta scales by observing what works elsewhere and implementing it.

subtitles.text: Google wasn't the first search engine. Facebook wasn't the first social network. Tesla wasn't the first electric car company.
The secret about Silicon Valley and innovation here is that every idea is actually a small evolution into a previous idea.
So the best entrepreneurs, what they do: they look at the ideas that already exist and they find a small twist,
a small difference, a small increment that makes it better than it was before, and then they run from it.
Meta is the largest social media company in the world. It didn't invent the Reels, it didn't invent Stories,
it didn't invent Instagram. They just see what's working and they copy it.

Business.title: Business Advice from a Shark Tank Investor. 💼📊
Business.description: Kevin O'Leary, a prominent investor on Shark Tank, shares a hard-hitting piece of business advice regarding employee termination. He explains that when multiple stakeholders—customers, shareholders, or employees—consistently voice concerns about a specific leader, it's a clear signal from the market that a change is necessary. He emphasizes the importance of listening to these external signals over internal attempts to 'fix' the situation.

subtitles.text: The reason I know it's time to terminate somebody in one of my companies,
is when my customers and my shareholders call me three times about that person.
It's always the same. It's always the same.
When a customer is complaining about a CEO, or a head of sales, or the head of R&D,
or employees are not happy, and I keep getting that phone call,
I get on the plane and I get my whacking stick.
Because there's something wrong.
And no matter how you try and fix it, the market's talking to you.

Business.title: $12B CEO biggest advice for Entrepreneurs
Business.description: In this clip, Henrique Dubugras, co-founder of Brex, explains why his company eliminated traditional 'people managers'. Using Jony Ive's work at Apple as an example, he argues that management should be integrated with the work itself rather than being a separate, non-productive role.

subtitles.text: So we eliminated all the people managers in the company.
A lot of companies get bigger and then you have managers whose role is to run a little team of eight people that would the go to do something.
This is just wrong. This is broken because there's no way to manage people separate from managing the work itself.
It's like thinking of Jony Ive at Apple. You think he's managing the team that then designs the iPhone?
No, Jony Ive designed the iPhone himself. There's a team of people helping him do it.
He manages them in service of the work versus as a separate thing entirely.

Daily Life.title: Day in the Life of a College Student at Penn State University on a Tuesday #pennstate
Daily Life.description: A student takes viewers through his busy Tuesday at Penn State University. From picking an outfit and attending five different classes starting at 9:05 AM to joining a Reach Club meeting with guest speaker Katie Feeney, the video showcases typical campus life. The day concludes with a walk around campus, watching a Phillies game, and winding down in a LED-lit dorm room.

subtitles.text: Day in the life of a college student at Penn State University on a Tuesday.
I picked out my outfit, I hopped in my Zoom class at 9:05.
Got on the bus, went to my second class that started at 10:35.
Third class at 12:05, fourth class at 1:35.
And then my last class was at 3:05, it ended at 4:20. I went home for a little bit.
Hopped on the bus, went to a Reach Club meeting, social media club. They had a guest speaker, Katie Feeney.
Then I walked around campus for a little bit as I waited for my roommate to pick me up, looking at the sights.
And then when we got back, we watched the Phillies and I went to bed.

Daily Life.title: Everything I ate today as a college student at GCU
Daily Life.description: Nico Felich, a college student graduating in three weeks, takes viewers through his daily routine and everything he eats. His day includes early morning classes at Grand Canyon University, quick snacks like honey and Pop-Tarts, a lunch break at Potbelly, and a 7-mile run before ending the night with oatmeal and a movie.

subtitles.text: Everything I ate today as a college student who graduates in three weeks. I woke up at 7:00 AM, but i wanted to sleep so bad, hopped in the shower.

threw on the fit some Lululemon joggers and a Nike knitted sweater, sprayed on some kog and then picked out my shoes for the day going with the Nike Invincible run didn't have time to grab breakfast so I just poured some honey in my mouth then

I made the long commute to school finally got there my first class is at 9:00 a.m. I have a test so I did some last minute Quizlet studying right before I was going to try to make it to lunch before eating but
I was so hungry so I grabbed a tar pop out of the vending machine curb stomped this and then headed into biochemistry at 11:00 sat through

Utterly brutal hour and 20 minutes of this then I waited for my Uber to come pick me up from school there she is then we went to go get lunch together I had a grilled chicken sandwich from pot belly then.
I got back to campus finished up a presentation I had in like 10 minutes after presenting and sitting through 3 hours of presentations
was done before heading home I grabbed some sushi on campus then I drove home at this point
I was so wiped but I wanted to get my run in for the day so I went for a little 7 mile run then
for a late night snack I had a big bowl of oatmeal watched some mall cop and then at 12:30 I got to bed.

Daily Life.title: Day in my Life at Community College 📚✏️
Daily Life.description: Landen Levine shares a typical day at a community college where most of his coursework is online. His routine involves sleeping in, hitting the gym with friends, doing laundry at his mom's house, and using a unique phone-locking method to focus on statistics homework. The day includes an in-person stats class and ends with hanging out with friends and finding free donuts.

subtitles.text: I go to a college with a 100% acceptance rate. Almost all my work is online, so I
get to sleep in until 9:00 and get ready for my first class by making some breakfast, which today was oatmeal.
And adding frozen berries makes it taste way better than fresh ones. I still don't technically have any in-person classes, and
maybe I should be doing work for my online classes, but I got a message from my friend asking if I wanted to go to the gym.
So, of course, I said yeah, and we hit abs together and got to do our favorite exercise.
Yes, mommy. But it was time to do some actual school work.
So, after heading to my mom's to do some laundry and steal some food, I actually needed to focus.
So, I locked my phone with this card and threw it away so I could sit at my desk and do some stats homework without getting distracted.
And actually then heading to stats class in person for a few hours because we have a test in a couple days, and I need to prepare for that.
It's open notes, which makes it a lot less stressful because it means I don't really have to study because all the formulas are written down.
So, since I didn't have to do any studying, I unblocked my phone and went to go hang out with some friends...
where we found these donuts that were going to get thrown away, and they looked so good. I haven't had a donut in forever, but it was actually insane.
And looking at this clip, I've realized I've become normalized to my friend just never ever wearing socks.
That about ended the day by walking a few miles and heading to...

Daily Life.title: HARVARD Day in the Life | 12 HOUR STUDY Day
Daily Life.description: Jack Anderson documents a high-intensity study day at Harvard University. Starting at 6:00 AM, he navigates various iconic campus locations including the Smith Campus Center, Widener Library, and Harvard Yard. The video follows his progress as he tackles 80 pages of reading, works on a prestigious scholarship application for a year in Beijing, and finishes a marathon 12-hour session late at night in Lamont Library.

subtitles.text: Come experience a brutal 12-hour study day at Harvard.
My day started at 6:00 a.m., and I'm headed straight for the Smith Campus Center.
The first thing I do is grab a coffee and then head straight into the student studying area...
where I work on my application for the prestigious Schwarzman Scholarship, which will allow me to spend a year learning from world leaders in Beijing.
Next, I'm headed over to the beautiful Widener Library.
With this beautiful architecture, it takes my breath away every single time.
For class tomorrow, I have roughly 80 pages of reading, so I spend the next few hours powering through those readings.
For a change of scenery, I decide to spend an hour studying outside in the Harvard Yard.
It's a beautiful spot with hundreds of tourists always passing by, and it's always a great way to reset.
Finally, to close out the day, I'm heading to the trenches of Lamont Library.
I spend the remainder of the night grinding out my assignments and scholarship applications until I'm at the point of falling asleep at my desk.

Daily Life.title: HARVARD PRE-MED *REALISTIC* DAY-IN-MY-LIFE
Daily Life.description: Elise Pham shares a brutally realistic day as a Harvard Pre-Med student. Her day starts at 4:30 AM with a hospital shift, followed by classes, long biology labs, and business calls for her personal brand. The video captures the exhausting reality of balancing high-level academics, work, and extracurriculars, ending with her falling asleep while studying at Lamont Library.

subtitles.text: Brutally realistic day in my life as a Harvard Premed student.
I woke at 4:30 a.m., not by voluntary choice because I was literally running on 3 hours of sleep,
but because I have a 5-hour shift at the hospital I work at.
Today was mainly just doing rounds of patient check-in, so I saw 20 patients before...
heading back in the rain, and I realized I forgot about my 12:00 p.m. class, so I ended up skipping that.
And I just headed straight to my biology lab, which is 4 hours every week.
Luckily, I have friends in my lab, or else I would completely lose my sanity.
But whenever I got out, it was completely dark, which was so sad.
I was super sleep deprived, but I had a business call with my marketing team because we were trying to revamp my website by the end of the year.
And then dinner today was not the best, so I ended up just eating stew over cold chicken.
Finally, I headed to Lamont Library to study for my organic chemistry class, but...
I ended up just falling asleep, which was definitely a new low for me.

Travel and Culture.title: Did You Know That France... 🤔
Travel and Culture.description: This educational short reveals intriguing geographical and historical facts about France. It covers France's extensive borders, its status as the country with the most time zones due to overseas territories, and the long-standing influence of the French language in England. Additionally, it highlights the high number of French speakers in Africa and the surprising origin of kilts.

subtitles.text: Things you didn't know about France.
France is bordering eight different countries through its metropolitan area.
It is also the country with the most time zones in the world, owning territories in 12 of the 24 time zones on the planet.
French was the official language in England for around 300 years.
In present, there are more French speakers in Africa than in Europe.
Kilts originated in France, not Scotland.

Travel and Culture.title: Don't make these mistakes in London!
Travel and Culture.description: In this travel short from DW Travel, Eva Janssen shares essential tips for tourists visiting London to avoid common faux pas. She covers frequently confused landmarks like London Bridge and Tower Bridge, explains tipping etiquette where service charges are often already included, warns against offensive hand gestures, and highlights the importance of standing on the right side of escalators on the Tube.

subtitles.text: It's the London Bridge! Things you should never do in London. Uh babe, that's the Tower Bridge.
This is the London Bridge. I knew that.
It's fine, a lot of tourists make that mistake. Thanks a lot, Fergie.
Tipping is normal at restaurants in Europe and the US, but a lot of restaurants in London have a service charge already included in the bill.
Make sure you check for it so you don't end up tipping twice.
Be careful when you're taking selfies in London 'cuz you just flipped people off in British.
Internationally, this is known as the peace sign, but if you turn that around, you could offend some people here.
Londoners are always in a rush on the Tube. If you don't want to get told off, make sure you stand on the right side of the escalator.
What other things should you never do in London?

Travel and Culture.title: Things that shocked me when I moved to Germany from Scotland
Travel and Culture.description: A Scots lass shares three major cultural differences she experienced after moving to Germany. She highlights the affordability and availability of alcohol in Berlin, the open sauna culture, and the contrast in healthcare costs regarding contraception compared to the free services in Scotland.

subtitles.text: Things that shocked me when I moved to Germany as a Scots lass.
Alcohol in Berlin is super cheap and you can buy it pretty much whenever you want.
In Scotland, it's illegal to buy alcohol in stores after 10 PM, and it's expensive!
Naked saunas. Seriously.
German spa culture is great, but let's be real, Scots are prudes.
The whole naked sauna thing took some serious getting used to.
You do have to pay for contraception, though.
In Scotland, everything is free, so you don't have to worry about whether you can afford it.
You can just choose the birth control that's best suited to you.

Travel and Culture.title: I Went to Every Country & Here’s My 7 Favorites!
Travel and Culture.description: After completing his journey to every country in the world, travel vlogger Drew Binsky reveals his top seven favorite destinations. He shares unique highlights for each, from the rare landscapes of Namibia and the transcontinental culture of Turkey to the unmatched hospitality in Afghanistan and the diverse adventures found in the Philippines.

subtitles.text: After visiting every country, here are my seven favorites on top of the world.
Number one: Namibia. This is the only place I've seen in the world where sand dunes meet the ocean.

Number two: Turkey. Istanbul is the only city in the world that is split between two continents, and the country overall is a unique blend of European and Asian cultures. Oh, and Turkish food is insanely delicious.
Number three: Mexico. Delicious food, warm people, beautiful beaches, and cheap prices. Oaxaca and Chiapas are my favorite states.
Number four: Venezuela. With the longest coastline in the Caribbean, Venezuela has crystal clear seawater with many beautiful islands and the nicest people that I met in South America.
Number five: Lebanon. Lebanon is one of the only places in the world where you can ski in the morning, skateboard in the afternoon, and go surfing at night.
Number six: Afghanistan. This country is home to the world's most hospitable people and a really rich culture that feels like you've entered a time machine back hundreds of years.
Number seven: The Philippines. The land of adventures: scuba diving, hiking, street food, jeepneys, lechon, and simply just more fun in the sun.

Travel and Culture.title: The Benefits of TRAVELING.
Travel and Culture.description: Actor Bryan Cranston reflects on the profound benefits of travel, emphasizing the wisdom gained through exploration and getting lost. He discusses how travel forces social interaction and heightened awareness of one's environment—from navigating new places to being mindful of weather and local customs. Cranston suggests that through the process of exploring the unknown, one might ultimately find themselves.

subtitles.text: I think people gain a tremendous amount of wisdom when they travel, when they wander.
When they allow themselves, as I did, to get lost—and maybe you'll find yourself somewhere.
Traveling forces you to be social. You have to get directions and learn where things are; you're attuned to your environment.
You have to be more careful about the weather, your lodging, where you're going to eat, and how you'll get around.
When you're there, what is it you want to see? How can you find a place for you to actually just rest?
It's an experience like no other. I love to travel, just kind of exploring different places.
As long as you have a sense of direction, you'll find your way. You'll figure it out.

Movies and TV Shows.title: One of the greatest monologues in film #fightclub
Movies and TV Shows.description: This short video features Tyler Durden's iconic 'middle children of history' monologue from the 1999 film, Fight Club. In this powerful scene, Tyler speaks to his followers about the existential frustration of a generation without a 'great war' or 'great depression,' arguing that their struggle is a spiritual one against consumerism and societal expectations.

subtitles.text: God damn it, an entire generation pumping gas, waiting tables; slaves with white collars.
Advertising has us chasing cars and clothes, working jobs we hate so we can buy shit we don't need.
We're the middle children of history, man. No purpose or place. We have no Great War. No Great Depression.
Our Great War is a spiritual war... our Great Depression is our lives.

Movies and TV Shows.title: Why should I always be the one to change?
Movies and TV Shows.description: In this powerful scene from the play *Prodigal Son*, a young student (played by Timothée Chalamet) confronts his headmaster about societal expectations and the pressure to change. He questions why he is judged before having the chance to discover his identity and references philosophers like Plato and Socrates to emphasize his point about fearlessness and respect.

subtitles.text: Everybody talks to me like I'm the one, you know, I should change.
Why should I change? I've never even gotten to find out who I am and you want me to change. That's crazy.
You tell me I'm bad before I even get to be anything. What the hell is that? Original sin or something?
I read Plato while on a park bench in the Bronx. Let me tell you something.
Plato, he wasn't afraid. Diogenes, he wasn't afraid. Socrates, they wasn't afraid of anything. They were men.
Why are you the headmaster and I'm the student? Do you understand? I have to earn your respect,
but you don't have to earn mine. What is that?

Movies and TV Shows.title: Thanos was trying to save Earth || Eternals X Infinity War
Movies and TV Shows.description: This fan-made edit explores the theory that Thanos's snap in 'Infinity War' was an attempt to delay the Emergence of a Celestial, as seen in Marvel's 'Eternals'. By reducing the human population, the energy required for the Celestial Tiamut to grow would have been delayed, potentially saving Earth from total destruction. The video features cinematic clips and dialogue from both films, set to a slowed version of 'Interlinked'.

subtitles.text: Planet Earth was chosen to host the Celestial Tiamut.
In order to grow, Tiamut needs vast amounts of energy from intelligent life.
Five years ago, Thanos erased half of the population of the universe.
But the people of this planet brought everyone back with the snap of a finger.
It needs correction. You don't know that; I'm the only one who knows that.
The human population of this planet has reached the required amount.
It is time for the Emergence to begin.
I'm the only one with the will to act on it.
The hardest choices require the strongest wills.

Movies and TV Shows.title: Tony's Final Speech | Avengers Endgame
Movies and TV Shows.description: This poignant edit features Tony Stark's recorded farewell message from 'Avengers: Endgame'. Set to the slowed song 'Kamin', the video blends Tony's reflections on life, family, and the risks of heroism with emotional clips of his fellow Avengers and his daughter, Morgan. It culminates in his iconic 'I love you 3000' and a final shot of the original arc reactor from Iron Man 1.

subtitles.text: Everybody wants a happy ending, right? But it doesn't always roll that way. I'm hoping if you play this back, it's in celebration.
I hope families are reunited, I hope we get it back, and something like a normal version of the planet has been restored.
If there ever was such a thing. So I thought I'd probably better record a little greeting in the case of an untimely death.
On my part. I mean, not that death at any time isn't untimely.
This time travel thing we're going to try and pull off tomorrow, it's got me scratching my head about the survivability of it.
Then again, that's the hero gig. Part of the journey is the end.
What am I even trippin' for? Everything's gonna work out exactly the way it's supposed to.
I love you 3000.

Movies and TV Shows.title: Harvey’s Ultimate Power Move: Trading Mike Over a RECORD!
Movies and TV Shows.description: In this scene from the TV show 'Suits', Harvey Specter uses Mike Ross as a bargaining chip in a wager with Louis Litt. After Louis refuses to admit Harvey is the best closer in New York, Harvey surprisingly hands Mike over to Louis for one case, hinting at a hidden agenda involving a record and a specific legal strategy.

subtitles.text: Um, I am taking Mike exclusively for the next 2 weeks.
Excuse me? Our wager, the prize sisters, my triumph. Does that ring a bell?
Can't have him. Just say out loud that I'm the best closer in New York and you can have him.
Fine. To Donna.
No. Then we don't have a deal.
That was fun. Morning. If that's a big check, I want another Marne bag. Hands off the vinyl, Junior.
10:00. Nice of you to join us.
Was closing Cranson this morning.
Oh, yeah. Couldn't get it done by 9:00, huh? You're a little lippy for a Monday.
Whoa. Come here, Lippy. I want to show you something. Oh, yeah. What? You got Bieber fever?
What? He's all yours.
What? Why the sudden change of mind?
Does it matter?
Duration? One case? Jurisdiction? Exclusive.
What can I do to him?
What do you want to do to him? Honor the boundaries of labor law.
I prefer the Geneva Convention.
Then we have a deal.
I think I need a sick day.

Movies and TV Shows.title: 🚛 Optimus Prime’s Iconic Final Speech | Transformers (2007)
Movies and TV Shows.description: The closing scene of the 2007 Transformers movie features Optimus Prime delivering a message to the surviving Autobots. He speaks about the courage of humanity and invites his fellow Transformers to join him on Earth, declaring 'We are here, we are waiting.'

subtitles.text: I have witnessed their capacity for courage and though we are worlds apart, like us, there's more to them than meets the eye.
I am Optimus Prime, and I send this message to any surviving Autobots taking refuge among the stars.
We are here. We are waiting.

Movies and TV Shows.title: But I believed in you... | Batman Begins
Movies and TV Shows.description: A powerful scene from 'Batman Begins' featuring a confrontation between Bruce Wayne and his former mentor, Ra's al Ghul. Ra's reminds Bruce of his training and expresses disappointment that Bruce isn't standing by his side, while Bruce affirms his commitment to protecting the people of Gotham.

subtitles.text: When I found you in that jail, you were lost. But I believed in you. I took away your fear...
...and I showed you a path. You are my greatest student. It should be you standing by my side saving the world.
I am standing where I belong. Between you and the people of Gotham.

Movies and TV Shows.title: The Price No One Talks About
Movies and TV Shows.description: A dramatic edit featuring dialogue from 'The Dark Knight' and 'The Dark Knight Returns'. Bruce Wayne and Alfred Pennyworth discuss the unforeseen consequences of Batman's actions in Gotham, highlighting the heavy personal toll—loss of peace of mind and one's former identity—that comes with standing in front to inspire change.

subtitles.text: I was meant to inspire good, not madness, not death.
You have inspired good, but you spat in the faces of Gotham's criminals. Didn't you think there might be some casualties?
Things were always going to get worse before they got better.
And the one who stands in front always pays more than the rest.
Sometimes the price is your peace of mind. Sometimes it's who you used to be.

Anime/Cartoons.title: Dive, dive, dive! - Jonny Quest
Anime/Cartoons.description: A classic scene from the science fiction animation Jonny Quest. Dr. Benton Quest and the crew investigate a mysterious explosion at sea, speculating whether it was caused by a remnant of the war. The video captures the suspense and high-stakes atmosphere characteristic of the retro series.

subtitles.text: What do you suppose that explosion was from, Doctor? It could have been from an old mine left over from the war.
But then again, I want armed lookouts posted fore and aft. Is that right?
Tomorrow's the big day.

Anime/Cartoons.title: King & Queens | Dr. Stone Edit
Anime/Cartoons.description: This dynamic edit captures a pivotal and high-stakes moment from the anime Dr. Stone. As humanity faces a recurring petrification calamity, Senku and his allies must make a desperate gamble using their scientific knowledge. The video showcases the emotional weight of their sacrifices and the intense determination to save humankind from a second era of stone.

subtitles.text: We've suffered heavy losses and this is the only chance we have left. This measure is our best and last hope.
The calamity that turned humankind to stone 3,700 years ago... it's happening again, but this time by our own science.
We're in another nasty turning point in history. We're destroying humanity, and then we're going to save it.
Don't let him get everything. On the door!
[Intense Music and Action]

Anime/Cartoons.title: Gojo Edit | "Reverse Cursed Technique..."
Anime/Cartoons.description: This intense edit features Gojo Satoru from Jujutsu Kaisen, specifically highlighting the moment he masters the Reverse Cursed Technique. The video captures the desperate situation on the verge of death where Gojo shifts his focus from counterattacking to regenerating his body. The scene transitions into a visually striking sequence with a modern soundtrack, showcasing Gojo's newfound power and confidence.

subtitles.text: Cursed energy is negative energy. While it can enhance the body, it can't regenerate it.
So you multiply that negative energy against itself to create positive energy. I gave up on counterattacking and poured all my focus into the reverse cursed technique.
Easier said than done. I could never do that until now. Is he high? But I finally grasped it on the verge of...
Death.

Anime/Cartoons.title: Shazam QUIT Justice League?
Anime/Cartoons.description: In this powerful scene from Justice League Unlimited, Shazam (Captain Marvel) addresses the Justice League members after a heated conflict. He expresses his deep disappointment in his former idols, especially Superman, stating that they no longer act like the heroes he once looked up to. Shazam emphasizes his belief in fair play and the benefit of the doubt, ultimately deciding to quit the League because he feels they have lost their moral compass.

subtitles.text: Look, Captain... I wanted no more lectures. I called this meeting and I'm going to have my say.
But my whole life I've looked up to the League. You were my heroes, every one of you.
And you... you were more than a hero. I idolized you. I wanted to be you.
Whenever I was out there facing down the bad guys, I'd think, 'What would Superman do?' Now I know.
I believe in fair play. I believe in taking people at their word and giving them the benefit of the doubt.
Back home, I've come up against my share of pretty nasty bad guys, but I never had to act the way they did to win a fight.
I always found another way. I guess I'm saying I... I like being a hero, a symbol.
And that's why I'm quitting the Justice League. You don't act like heroes anymore.

Anime/Cartoons.title: Most Emotional Quote From Kung Fu Panda
Anime/Cartoons.description: In this emotional scene from Kung Fu Panda, Po confronts Shifu about why he didn't quit despite the harsh treatment. Po expresses his pain and explains that staying was worth it because he believed Shifu could help him change and become someone more than himself. The video includes a dramatic soundtrack and clips from the movie to emphasize the emotional impact of the dialogue.

subtitles.text: And why did you quit? You know, I was trying to get rid of you. Yet you stayed.
Yeah... I stayed. I stayed because every time you threw a brick at my head or said I smelled, it hurts.
But it could never hurt more than it did every day of my life just being me. I stayed...
Because I thought if anyone could change me, could make me NOT me, it was you.

Anime/Cartoons.title: This comeback is personal. 😤
Anime/Cartoons.description: A powerful motivational short featuring Zenitsu Agatsuma from Demon Slayer. The video juxtaposes the harsh criticism and self-doubt Zenitsu faced with his inner strength and determination to prove his worth. It serves as a reminder to never give up, even when others walk away, and to make your comeback personal as an apology to yourself for the times you doubted your own potential.

subtitles.text: This comeback is more than personal. It's an apology to myself.
The way you cry like a baby all day long... aren't you ashamed of yourself? Good for nothing.
Any effort spent on you is an absolute waste of time. I hate even looking at you. So get out!
Why are you even here? Why do you insist on clinging on like a patch of mold?
If you mess up or cry, run away even once, everyone says 'Oh, this guy's no good' and walks away.
It's alright to cry or go off by yourself. Just don't ever give up. Believe in yourself.
It's been a while, hasn't it, Zenitsu?
No matter what stands before me, no matter what it may take...
This is something I have to do.

Anime/Cartoons.title: Asta Never give up motivational Speech🗡️
Anime/Cartoons.description: A short, powerful motivational clip featuring Asta from Black Clover. The speech addresses the pain of betrayal, the failure to protect loved ones, and the loss of belief, ending with a firm command to never give up again.

subtitles.text: I get it? You were tricked. You couldn't protect the ones you loved and everything you once believed in was lost.
You're dealing with so many crazy feelings you can't stand it anymore. That's why I have to tell you this:
Don't you dare give up again!

Anime/Cartoons.title: My hair has a strange magic power.
Anime/Cartoons.description: A short clip from the animated movie Tangled. Rapunzel demonstrates the magical healing power of her hair to Flynn Rider by singing a specific incantation while wrapping her glowing hair around his injured hand. The scene reveals that she has been kept in a tower her whole life because her mother wanted to protect and control this rare magic.

subtitles.text: I have magic hair that glows when I sing!
Flower, gleam and glow. Let your power shine.
-Her hair glows. -It doesn't just glow.
So, you're being strangely cryptic as you wrap your magic hair around my injured hand.
Flower, gleam and glow. Change the Fates' design.
Please don't freak out! I'm not freaking out. Are you freaking out? No, I'm just very interested in your hair...
...and the magical qualities that it possesses. How long has it been doing that exactly?
Mother says when I was a baby, people tried to cut it. They wanted to take it for themselves.
That's why Mother never let me... You never left that tower.

Music Lyrics.title: WRONG - Chris Grey
Music Lyrics.description: This atmospheric edit features the song 'WRONG' by Chris Grey. It blends various beautiful and dark-themed cityscapes and landscapes from around the world, including Istanbul, New York, and Rio de Janeiro. The visuals are enhanced with aesthetic filters and on-screen lyrics, creating a moody and introspective vibe that complements the song's emotional tone.

subtitles.text: Baby, tell me why doesn't lovin' you feel wrong?
Oh, it hurts until it don't.
I can't stay away for long.
I don't know how to get you off my mind.
You make Hell our paradise.
Lovin' you is suicide.
And I do it every night.

Music Lyrics.title: Lana Del Rey - Diet Mountain Dew | Lyrics | Status
Music Lyrics.description: A stylized lyrics edit of Lana Del Rey's 'Diet Mountain Dew'. The video features a high-energy aesthetic with fast-paced cuts and rhythmic text overlays that sync with the song's 'roller coaster' theme. It captures the song's blend of infatuation and danger, set against an atmospheric backdrop.

subtitles.text: Maybe I like this roller coaster.
Maybe it keeps me high.
Maybe the speed, it brings me closer.
I could sparkle up your eye.
Hurt me and tell me you're mine.
I don't know why, but I like it.
Scary? My God, you're divine.
Gimme them, gimme them dope and Diet Mountain Dew, .
New York City, never was there ever a girl so pretty.
Do you think we'll be in love forever? Do you think we'll be in love?

Music Lyrics.title: The Weeknd - Blinding Lights (Lyrics)
Music Lyrics.description: A short, atmospheric lyrics video for The Weeknd's hit song 'Blinding Lights'. The video features a dark, aesthetic style with a crescent moon peeking through clouds and a misty forest road, perfectly capturing the song's nighttime vibe.

subtitles.text: Sin City's cold and empty
No one's around to judge me
I can't see clearly when you're gone
Oh-oh
I said, ooh
I'm blinded by the lights
No, I can't sleep till I feel your
touch

Music Lyrics.title: Astrid S - Hurts So Good (Slowed Lyrics)
Music Lyrics.description: This aesthetic short features a slowed-down version of Astrid S's 'Hurts So Good'. The video presents a moody, late-night cityscape with the lyrics displayed in a classic, elegant font. It captures the melancholic yet catchy vibe of the track, focusing on the internal conflict of a complicated relationship.

subtitles.text: It's complicated.
So complicated.
When it hurts...
But it hurts so good.
Do you take it?
Do you break it off?
When it hurts...
But it hurts so good.
Can you say it? Can you say it?
Your love is like...
Hey-na, na-na-na-na. Your love is like... Hey-na, na-na-na-na
It hurts so good. Hey-na, na-na-na-na.

Music Lyrics.title: Without Me - Halsey (Lyrics)
Music Lyrics.description: An atmospheric lyrics video for Halsey's hit 'Without Me'. The short features moody, nocturnal landscapes—dark mountains, a moonlit beach, and deep blue forests—that mirror the song's themes of devotion and heartbreak. Each frame is carefully synchronized with the lyrics to create a powerful visual experience.

subtitles.text: Found you when your heart was broke
I filled your cup until it overflowed
Took it so far to keep you close
I was afraid to leave you on your own
I said I'd catch you if you fall

Music Lyrics.title: Timeless - The Weeknd, Playboi Carti (Lyrics)
Music Lyrics.description: A lyric short for the track 'Timeless' featuring The Weeknd and Playboi Carti. The song explores themes of status, self-assurance, and a lifestyle of excess, with the artists declaring their influence and success as 'timeless' regardless of external opinions.

subtitles.text: If I was you, I would cut up my wrist. XO tatted all over her body, she just want to roll it.
I don't mind it. Ever since I was a jit, I've been legit... Oh, you should let her go, she want to...
...be Double-O tatted on her body. It don't matter what they say, I'm timeless.

Music Lyrics.title: All the stars - SZA,Kendrick Lamar(lyrics)
Music Lyrics.description: An aesthetic space-themed lyric edit of the song 'All The Stars' by Kendrick Lamar and SZA. The video features visually striking shots of planets, nebulae, and galaxies, synchronized with the track's lyrics to evoke a sense of wonder and dreams.

subtitles.text: Maybe the... my dreams...
Might let me know...
All the stars are closer.
All the stars are closer.
All the stars are closer.
Maybe the... my dreams...
Might let me know...
All the stars are closer.
All the stars are closer.
All the stars are closer.

Music Lyrics.title: ruth b - dandelions lyrics song
Music Lyrics.description: A poetic lyric edit for the song 'Dandelions' by Ruth B. The video features cinematic, dark-toned visuals with expressive typography, capturing the emotional depth of the lyrics as they express deep affection and the joy found in a partner's smile.

subtitles.text: Wishing on everyone that you'd be mine... mine...
I see forever in your eyes. I feel okay when I see you smile...
Smile...

Music Lyrics.title: Tell Your Friend She's Next In Line
Music Lyrics.description: An aesthetic lyric edit of a slowed-down version of the song 'RENEGADE'. The video features a dark, moody beach setting under a stormy sky with lightning, perfectly matching the mysterious and somewhat provocative nature of the lyrics. The expressive text overlays follow the rhythm of the track, creating a strong visual-auditory mood.

subtitles.text: Keep your eyes on mine...
And if you want I'll tell you lies...
Tell you I'm yours for life.
And tell your friend she's next in line.

Music Lyrics.title: Lady Gaga- Bloody Mary
Music Lyrics.description: An atmospheric lyric edit featuring Lady Gaga's 'Bloody Mary' set against a dark, moody seascape. The video's deep blue and grey tones create a powerful 'Winter Arc' aesthetic, focusing on themes of resilience and self-reliance as the lyrics reflect an attitude of moving forward without looking back.

subtitles.text: I won't cry for you.
I won't crucify the things you do.
I won't cry for you, see...
When you're gone, I'll still be...
Bloody Mary.

Music Lyrics.title: Careless Whisper - George Michael #lyrics #song #musicvideo #trending #shorts
Music Lyrics.description: An aesthetic lyric edit for the legendary song 'Careless Whisper' by George Michael. The video uses a minimalist color palette of cream and deep red, featuring stylized text that flows with the emotional tempo of the music. It captures the melancholic essence of regret and lost love, making it a perfect piece for fans of classic hits and visual edits.

subtitles.text: I wish that we could lose this crowd.
Maybe it's better this way.
We'd hurt each other with the things we want to say.
We could have been so good together.
We could have lived this dance forever.
But now who's gonna dance with me?
Please stay.

Interviews.title: How to Crush a Job Interview
Interviews.description: In this short video from The Elev8 Podcast, a resume writer shares a game-changing tip for job interviews. He advises against the common mistake of saying you have no questions at the end of an interview. Instead, he suggests asking a powerful question: 'If you hire me today, how will you know in a year's time that I was the right fit?' This question helps the interviewer visualize you thriving in the role, making you a more memorable candidate.

subtitles.text: I write resumes for a lot of people. The advice I give to everybody—every employer always asks this question:
'Do you have any questions?' They always ask it at the end. People always go, 'Oh, no, I think you've covered everything,' trying to be polite.
Don't ever do that. The question you ask is, 'If you hire me today, how will you know in a year's time that I was the right fit?'
And it does a few things. The first thing it does is makes them visualize you, not only in the role, but thriving.
And it really does work!

Interviews.title: Michael Olise is the KING of the post-match interview 😂 👑
Interviews.description: Michael Olise delivers a hilariously brief post-match interview. When asked to talk through his goal, he simply states: 'Wilf passed me the ball. Shot. Scored.' His nonchalant and direct responses to the interviewer's questions highlight his unique and entertaining personality.

subtitles.text: Just talk us through it.
Through what?
The goal.
I think Wilf passed me the ball.
Shot. Scored.
Nice and brief. But it was a moment that captured the game, that won the game.
And what's the feeling like when the ball does hit the back of the net?
It was a good feeling.
Do you feel you deserved it overall?
Yeah.

Interviews.title: Jurgen Klopp HITS BACK at reporter
Interviews.description: In a tense post-match press conference, Liverpool manager Jurgen Klopp has a sharp exchange with a reporter. When the reporter quotes former player Dietmar Hamann, suggesting Liverpool 'need a spark,' Klopp dismisses Hamann's opinion and tells the reporter to ask their own question instead of relying on someone else's phrases. The clip ends with Klopp playfully challenging the reporter to ask a question without using the word 'spark.'

subtitles.text: I heard the phrase after the game against Arsenal used that Liverpool need a 'spark.' What do you feel you need?
Who said that? Didi Hamann? Oh, great. He's a fantastic source. Well respected everywhere.
Former Liverpool midfielder, obviously achieved a lot. That doesn't give you the right to say what you want, especially when you have no idea.
Well, I'm asking you, what do you feel that you need? Where do you see a spark, if it's a spark that you need?
I actually think Didi Hamann doesn't deserve that you use his phrase to ask me a question. Do me a favor and ask your own question.
Okay, that's good. We won't use that phrase then. But what is it you feel that you need?
Try to ask a question without the word 'spark.' That's the challenge now, huh? No 'spark' in there.

Interviews.title: How to Interview While Still Employed
Interviews.description: This video from the channel A Life Engineered provides practical advice for managing job interviews while currently employed. It emphasizes breaking down the overwhelming task of interviewing into smaller, more manageable subcomponents. Additionally, it suggests forming or joining a support group with colleagues who are also looking for new opportunities, as this can provide valuable mock interviews and maintain accountability throughout the process.

subtitles.text: Go find your co-workers that also want to get a new job.
And you were preparing for interviews and doing interviews while you were still employed. How did you manage your time?
I find that when you take a gigantic task and break it down into smaller subcomponents, it makes it more realistic to actually accomplish those subtasks.
Also, I would say, um, I happened to have some friends who were similarly interviewing. So we kind of had like a support group, study sessions, however you want to classify it...
where we would give each other mock interviews, and that really helped maintain accountabilities.

Interviews.title: Day in a life of Tim Cook - CEO of Apple
Interviews.description: This short video provides a glimpse into the daily routine of Apple CEO Tim Cook. He discusses his early morning habits, including rising between 4:00 AM and 5:00 AM, spending his first hour on emails, and his dedicated workout routine. Cook emphasizes the importance of focus during his gym sessions and expresses his passion for collaborating with inspiring colleagues at the Apple office.

subtitles.text: Day in the life of Tim Cook. I get up really early, like at 4:00 to 5:00.
I spend my first hour doing email, and I'm pretty religious about doing this. I read emails from a lot of customers and employees.
Before I go into the office, I go work out. I spend an hour in the gym, usually doing strength training.
I do no work during that period of time at all. I never check my phone. I'm just totally focused on working out.
And after doing that, after showering etc., I go into the office and I start working with people I love.
And it's an incredible feeling to work with people that bring out the best in you.

Academic.title: What Is Quantum? | Michio Kaku | Neil deGrasse Tyson
Academic.description: In this enlightening clip from StarTalk, physicist Michio Kaku simplifies the concept of 'quantum' for Neil deGrasse Tyson and the audience. He explains that while we often think of energy as continuous, 'quantum' actually means energy is 'chopped up' into discrete pieces or particles called photons. Kaku highlights how quantum physics has transformed our understanding of the universe, replacing the idea of continuous energy with a world of interacting energy particles.

subtitles.text: Explain quantum, just the word.
When you look at energy, we think of energy as being continuous, uninterrupted.
Quantum means you chop it up. That they're pieces of the quantum. And these pieces of the quantum of energy are called photons.
All the universe is based on particles, nothing continuous. So the world of continuous energy has been replaced by quantum physics.
The physics of particles of energy that interact with other particles of energy.

Academic.title: India’s air pollution is deadly. Bryan shares his research and insights on this urgent crisis.
Academic.description: In this urgent discussion, Bryan Johnson shares his research on India's air quality crisis. He describes his experience in Mumbai, where PM 2.5 levels reached 20 times the recommended limit. Bryan emphasizes that improving air quality in India could have a greater positive impact than curing cancer. He notes the lack of local awareness and filtration systems, prompting his team to research the severe health impacts of such high pollution levels.

subtitles.text: Improving air quality in India is better than curing cancer. The biggest contributors to pollution in India are PM 2.5 and PM 10.
In Mumbai, we carried an air filter with us everywhere we went. We set it up in the place we were at.
What was the PM 2.5 there in Mumbai? Outside it was between 80 and 100.
So it was almost 20 times the recommended limit. Yeah, air quality has been in the news in India the last couple of months.
Every year around winter time, especially up north, the air quality really goes off the charts.
It becomes some of the worst in the world. Sometimes it takes a tourist to see something.
When we came here, we had prepared with masks and filters, and then we found that nobody paid attention to the air quality anywhere.
Nobody had filtration devices; nobody had any measurements. So we tried to build our own systems.
We started doing research when we came and said, 'Okay, what impact does bad air quality have?' And we were shocked to find the results.

Academic.title: The Problem with Geothermal Energy
Academic.description: This short from Real Engineering explores the immense potential and the significant challenges of geothermal energy. While the Earth's core generates enough heat to power humanity for millions of years, accessing it remains difficult because temperatures only rise about 30°C per kilometer. The video explains the efficiency of supercritical steam, which forms at extreme depths, and why current drilling techniques are too expensive to reach those levels in most parts of the world.

subtitles.text: Deep within the Earth, nuclear decay generates immense quantities of heat. The Earth's core is 6,000°C.
Just 0.1% of the planet's heat content could supply all of our energy needs for 2 million years. However, accessing that heat is not easy.
Some countries like Iceland are lucky, with geothermal energy reaching close to the surface. So close that geysers of boiling water erupt from the ground.
Areas like this are rare, which is why geothermal energy accounts for a tiny fraction of the world's energy supply.
We have a safe fission reactor under our feet, but we simply can't access it easily.
The deeper you go, the hotter it gets, with temperatures increasing roughly 30°C per kilometer.
The hotter the temperature, the more energy we can extract, especially if we can create supercritical steam.
Steam turns supercritical at roughly 374°C and 22 megapascals of pressure.

Supercritical steam has some special characteristics that make it highly efficient at driving turbines, but problem is reaching those depths of supercritical steam is basically impossible in most locations.
Current drilling techniques are limited because the cost of drilling increases exponentially with depth.
One company is trying to change that. I visited their facility in Houston, Texas, to check out their progress on the Real Engineering channel.

Academic.title: Why Do Time Zones Look So Strange 🌍 How They Work 🕒
Academic.description: This educational short explains the logic and irregularities of global time zones. It details how time is measured from the Prime Meridian (UTC 0) and why time zone boundaries are often zig-zagged rather than straight lines to avoid splitting cities or regions into different hours. The video also highlights interesting extremes, such as France having the most time zones (12) and China using only one time zone despite its massive geographical span.

subtitles.text: You probably know that the Earth is divided into 24 time zones, but why do they look so weird?
You see, time starts at the Prime Meridian, Greenwich, or Zero UTC (Coordinated Universal Time).
All zones east of Greenwich have positive offsets, while those to the west have negative offsets.
So, when you are in Los Angeles, you are in the UTC-8 time zone.
But if the time zones remained perfectly straight, there would be a number of inconveniences.
For example, in Phoenix, one part of the city would be in UTC-7 and another in -8, creating a 1-hour difference and significant confusion.
For this reason, it was decided to change the time zone boundaries based on the borders of countries and regions.
The country with the largest number of time zones is France; thanks to its numerous overseas departments, it covers 12 time zones.
At the same time, a giant like China technically covers five time zones but decided to use only one for the whole country.
As a result, western cities like Kashgar are still dark at 10:00 a.m.

Academic.title: How Does Gravity Really Work? | Explained in 48 Seconds.
Academic.description: In this short clip, Einstein's theory of gravity is simplified through a visual experiment. It demonstrates how mass, like a star, warps the fabric of space-time, causing objects to orbit or fall toward it. The video highlights that gravity isn't just a force, but a consequence of the curvature of space-time in a dimension we cannot directly see.

subtitles.text: What Einstein said was this: If you don't have anything out in space, that's a flat space.
If you were to take a planet, a ball, or a rocket ship and roll it, it would make a straight line in that space-time.
Watch it up on the screen: I roll it, and it makes a straight line.
But according to Einstein, if you have a star, that star will bend space-time in that extra dimension you can't see.
You can't see it bent up over there, but you can see it here.
And that bent space-time... if you roll the ball, see, now it looks like it's going around and around that planet.

News.title: BBC News apologises for 'error' in calling Israeli hostages ‘prisoners’
News.description: BBC News issues a formal apology for an error during their coverage of the release of three Israeli hostages. The broadcaster mistakenly referred to the hostages as ‘prisoners’ and corrected the terminology in this brief update, clarifying the situation regarding those due to be freed.

subtitles.text: And earlier today on BBC News, we reported on the release of the names of those three Israeli hostages
who are due to be freed tomorrow. At one point in our coverage,
we mistakenly called the hostages 'prisoners,' and we would like to apologise for that error.

subtitles.title: Donald Trump slams India, EU and other nations for high tariffs
subtitles.description: Donald Trump criticizes several nations, including India, China, and Mexico, for their high tariffs on US products. He states that the current system is unfair and announces that reciprocal tariffs will take effect on April 2.

subtitles.text: On average, the European Union, China, Brazil, India, Mexico, and Canada...
Have you heard of them?
And countless other nations charge us tremendously higher tariffs than we charge them.
It's very unfair. India charges us auto tariffs higher than 100%.
This is happening by friend and foe. This system is not fair to the United States and never was.
And so on April 2nd—I wanted to make it April 1st, but I didn't want to be accused of April Fools' Day.
April 2nd, reciprocal tariffs kick in.

News.title: “What Donald Trump felt at that moment... he's super irritated”
News.description: Michael Wolff, author and journalist, discusses Donald Trump's reaction during a speech by King Charles. Wolff suggests that Trump felt 'super irritated' by the King's words, which he interpreted as a 'direct slap' and an attempt to talk down to him, particularly regarding the war in Ukraine.

subtitles.text: I'm going to tell you what Donald Trump felt at that moment: he's super irritated to be by the King...
Today, as tyranny once again threatens Europe, we and our allies stand together in support of Ukraine.
This is—he's going to take that as a direct slap. He didn't look irritated, to be fair to President Trump, he looked up at the King but he didn't look irritated.
Well, I'm going to tell you what was going on behind those eyes. Yeah, I mean, to be told like that,
to be talked down like that, to essentially be corrected like that... I mean, he knows he has a problem here.
He can't end the war in Ukraine. This is a big issue with him.
And if he can't end that war as he promised, then his alternative is that that issue has to go.
